label: PASS
rationale: PowerCycle matches LockOnReset {0}, but the later Set disables WriteLockEnabled; with WriteLockEnabled false, WriteLocked is ignored, so write success is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: After PowerCycle, WriteLockEnabled is set false; the spec says WriteLocked is ignored when WriteLockEnabled is false, so rejecting the write is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: The final state has both read and write locking disabled, so no enabled lock is active; Level 0 Locked=0 follows the Locked-bit rule.

label: FAIL
rationale: Because ReadLockEnabled and WriteLockEnabled are both false at the target, any lock cells are disregarded/ignored; Level 0 Locked should be 0, not 1.

label: PASS
rationale: LockOnReset is {0,3}; after the host unlocks both cells, HardwareReset type 1 does not match, so the false lock cells are preserved.

label: FAIL
rationale: HardwareReset type 1 is not in LockOnReset {0,3}; the prior unlocked ReadLocked/WriteLocked values should remain false, not become true.

label: PASS
rationale: Programmatic reset is enabled and LockOnReset contains 3; TPER_RESET therefore sets ReadLocked and WriteLocked true, matching the Get result.

label: FAIL
rationale: With ProgrammaticResetEnable true and LockOnReset including 3, TPER_RESET must set ReadLocked and WriteLocked true; returning both false is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: PowerCycle sets WriteLocked true under LockOnReset {0}, but WriteLockEnabled is false, so write locking is ignored and the write may succeed.

label: FAIL
rationale: PowerCycle matches LockOnReset {0} and ReadLockEnabled is true, so ReadLocked becomes true and read requests must not be allowed; read success is not compliant.
